title: Godmanchester Stirling Memorial
crew_summary: 2 killed (5 survived); pilot Sqn Ldr. Drummond Wilson

external_narratives.summary: Page for Sqn Ldr Drummond Wilson and the crew of Stirling N3703 LS:Q, XV Squadron. On 10/11 April 1942, the aircraft was badly damaged by flak over Essen and crash-landed at Godmanchester after both starboard engines failed on approach to RAF Alconbury.
external_narratives.key_details: The crew were 'coned' by searchlights over Essen. On final approach to Alconbury, ordered to go around due to an aircraft blocking the runway. A damaged oil pipe broke, both starboard engines cut out. Navigator F/O Clifford Reeve was awarded the MBE for rescuing crew from the burning wreckage.

external_narratives.summary: Imperial War Museum register entry for the Short Stirling N3703 memorial at Godmanchester. Records the memorial stone commemorating the two crew members killed and the aircraft's operational loss.
external_narratives.key_details: Memorial reference 97973. Two killed: Sqn Ldr Wilson (pilot) and Sgt Edgar Gould (mid-upper gunner). Five survived but three died later in the war.

external_narratives.summary: Dedicated website for the Stirling N3703 crash at Godmanchester, including research by Roger Leivers who authored a book about the incident. Documents the crew's story, the crash, and the memorial dedication ceremony.
external_narratives.key_details: Roger Leivers researched and published a book about the crash. Dedication ceremony held with surviving relatives and military representatives.

local_contacts.role: Local authority responsible for memorial upkeep
